notes: LIVE read of the Basescan-verified Nockchain<->Base bridge on Base Sepolia (chainId 84532): MessageInbox.sol (DepositProcessed mints) + Nock.sol (BurnForWithdrawal burns), read-only via viem. Run with: export BASE_SEPOLIA_RPC_URL=...; npm run lab:base

Only the BASE-sourced subset of invariants runs live: quorum (CONTRACT-ENFORCED 3-of-5 — the on-chain verifier required >= threshold of the live bridgeNodes roster; the event does not name which signers, so per-signer identities are a scoped ecrecover follow-on), replay-safety, finality-depth, and finality-adequacy over real mints.

supply-conserved is intentionally NOT asserted: each Base mint is backed by a Nockchain-side burn that is NOT observable from Base, so the backing relation is not fabricated here. HTLC / multi-EVM / domain / challenge-window paths have no source on this single federated MessageInbox and do not run.

Honest limit: an empty event window (no deposits in the scanned range, or withdrawals disabled pre-launch) makes the live invariants pass VACUOUSLY — that is a real read of 'nothing happened', NOT an 'app works' proof. The report's adapter observation carries the observed mint/burn event counts.

A successful live read earns an app-report ONLY when bound to the deployed identity via app.baseDeploymentHash (sha256 of chainId+inbox+nock), exactly as a kernel run requires kernelHash. See docs/xchain-security-model.md.

steps.title: Read the live Base Sepolia bridge deployment (read-only viem)
steps.expectation: Base RPC reachable and the MessageInbox/Nock contracts readable

invariants.title: Every live mint carries contract-enforced >= 3-of-5 authorized attestation
Each live deposit (txId) is minted at most once
Live mints only after >= requiredConfirmations on Base
Live mints meet Base Sepolia's registry finality floor + basis (not a reversible soft-confirm)
